The Indian financial sector aggressively processed a massive, historic milestone achieved during the week's trading. The Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) Sensex had aggressively breached the psychological 6,000-point mark for the first time in history. ## SECTION 7 — This Day in History (World)
On February 12, 1809, two massive, fundamentally transformative figures in human history were born on the exact same day. Abraham Lincoln, the massive, highly revered 16th President of the United States, was born in Kentucky; he would successfully guide his nation through its massive Civil War and officially abolish slavery. Simultaneously, Charles Darwin, the massive, brilliant British naturalist, was born in England; his massive, highly controversial book *On the Origin of Species* fundamentally revolutionized the biological sciences by formally establishing the massive theory of evolution by natural selection.

On February 12, 1912, Puyi, the massive, heavily isolated boy-emperor of the Qing Dynasty, was forced to officially abdicate his throne. This massive, historic event formally ended over two millennia of massive, absolute imperial rule in China. The abdication was a massive, direct result of the Xinhai Revolution, establishing the Republic of China and plunging the massive, fragmented nation into decades of massive, devastating warlordism, civil war, and communist revolution.

## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (India)
On February 12, 1824, Maharishi Dayananda Saraswati, the massive, highly influential Indian philosopher, social leader, and founder of the Arya Samaj, was born in Gujarat. He heavily advocated for a massive, aggressive return to the teachings of the original Vedas, fiercely rejecting massive idolatry, caste discrimination, and untouchability. His massive, reformist ideology heavily influenced the development of modern Hindu nationalism and played a massive, foundational role in the early stages of the Indian independence movement.

On February 12, 1922, Mahatma Gandhi made the massive, highly controversial, and profoundly significant decision to officially and aggressively halt the massive Non-Cooperation Movement. Deeply horrified by the horrific, massive violence at Chauri Chaura where protesters burned police officers alive, Gandhi strictly prioritized absolute non-violence (Ahimsa) over immediate political gains. His massive decision deeply fractured the Indian National Congress but heavily cemented his absolute moral authority over the massive Indian independence struggle.
